title: Knysna Oyster Festival kicks off with successful weekend
description: GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - The 41st Knysna...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - The 41st Knysna Oyster Festival kicked off on Friday 21 June 2024 with a huge successful weekend of sports, live entertainment, food and wine, and community events.


> According to Lungiswa Goya, spokesperson for the festival, the organising committee is delighted with the success of the festival so far and the number of attendees.


“We had a fantastic weekend in Knysna,” said Goya. “The town was full and the vibe was great. The Forest Marathon had 6,500 entries and the weather played ball so it was not too cold and this unique event did not disappoint.

“The Festival Hub on the Knysna High School sport field was packed with runners and festival-goers on Saturday, many of whom watched the Springboks thrash Wales live in the Castle Lite tent. Afterwards they were entertained by Ard Matthews.

“The Fine Wine Festival on Saturday night at Simola, the Thesen Islands Oyster Fest Fair, the Miss Pearl Beauty Pageant and the Eden Classic Car Show in Sedgefield were all a huge success and we’ve had great feedback from both the organisers and attendees.“We’re very happy about how the festival is going and looking forward to some great events this week as well as the Knysna Cycle Tour this coming weekend.”

Highlights of this week include the Oyster Roast event at the Festival Hub. Dubbed Oysters: Roasted, Dressed or Naked? the event will offer guests the opportunity to taste oysters naked or prepared in different delectable ways.

The event includes the Oyster Shucking Competition in which shuckers will chase the world record of 38 oysters shucked in one minute, followed by some great comedy by Kurt Schoonraad.

Entrance to the Festival Hub on the Knysna High School sport field is FREE.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
